{
  "markdown": "Cursor\n\nIntegrations›Cursor\n\nSpecStory integrates seamlessly with [Cursor](https://cursor.com/). Cursor already helps developers move faster with inline edits, multi-line completions, and codebase chat. SpecStory adds the missing layer by capturing why the changes were made.\n\nWith SpecStory enabled, every AI conversation in Cursor is auto-saved as Markdown in your project. Your reasoning, tradeoffs, and design decisions live alongside your code: versioned, reusable, and shareable.\n\n## Install the SpecStory Extension\n\nVerify installation:\n\n- Open the Command Palette (&#96;Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+P&#96;).\n- Type&#96;SpecStory&#96;, the commands should appear.\n\nWarning\n\nIf you install SpecStory directly from the [Visual Studio Marketplace](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=SpecStory.specstory-vscode) website, you may end up with VS Code having the extension but not Cursor. Always install directly from inside Cursor to avoid this issue.\n\n## Verify It Works\n\n- Start a chat with Cursor’s AI.\n- Open your project folder.\n- You should now see a new&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; directory.\n- Inside, you should see a Markdown file of your session, named with a timestamp.\n\nTip\n\nSpecStory is local-first by default. Nothing leaves your machine unless you explicitly share it.\n\n## How SpecStory Enhances Cursor\n\n- Auto-save: Every AI conversations in Cursor are automatically stored in&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; as clean Markdown.\n- Manual saves: From the Command Palette, run&#96;SpecStory: Save AI Chat History&#96; to cherry-pick and combine conversations.\n- Share conversations with a URL: Use&#96;SpecStory: Share AI Chat History&#96; to generate a private, anonymous link at&#96;share.specstory.com&#96;.\n\n- Derived rules for AI: SpecStory can extract project-level rules and custom instructions from your chats, feeding them back into Cursor to improve suggestions.\n\nInfo\n\nRule derivation requires:\n\n- Auto-save enabled.\n- Signed-in SpecStory extension.\n\nInfo\n\nTool coverage includes Cursor tool v2 handlers (edit/read/terminal), plus Read/Write, Apply Patch, Grep, Glob, Ripgrep, Read Lints, and MultiEdit in Markdown exports.\n\n## Best Practices for Cursor Users\n\n- Commit your history: Include&#96;.specstory/history/&#96; in your repo so teammates can review reasoning in PRs.\n- Use manual saves before big changes: Preserve the conversation context before accepting multi-line or agent-driven edits.\n- Leverage derived rules: Cursor’s [Project Rules](https://docs.cursor.com/context/rules-for-ai#project-rules-recommended) evolve automatically when SpecStory is active.\n\n## Why This Matters\n\nCursor accelerates what changes in your codebase. SpecStory ensures you never lose why those changes happened. Together, they give you:\n\n- Faster debugging and onboarding\n- Traceable architectural decisions\n- Cleaner collaboration on AI-assisted code\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n- Extension mismatch: If VS Code shows SpecStory installed but Cursor doesn’t, uninstall and reinstall from Cursor’s built-in Marketplace.\n- No&#96;.specstory&#96; folder: Make sure you have an active project folder open. SpecStory only saves inside projects.\n- Still stuck? File an issue on [GitHub](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/issues) or contact [support@specstory.com](mailto:support@specstory.com).",
  "url": "https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/cursor"
}

[Exclude selected &#96;docs&#96; from current context · Issue #1463 · cursor/cursor](https://github.com/getcursor/cursor/issues/1463)\n\n# Multiple Files in Same Conversation - Cursor Issues\n\nThe search results reveal several reported issues related to multiple file instances and duplication in Cursor:\n\n## Multiple File Instances Bug\n\nCursor has a critical bug where the &#96;edit_file&#96; tool creates multiple instances of the same file simultaneously.[1] This occurs when files appear in both virtual paths (e.g., &#96;python:\\\\src\\\\test_tts.py&#96;) and actual paths (e.g., &#96;c:\\\\Proj\\\\Fami\\\\src\\\\test_tts.py&#96;), causing confusion about which instance is being edited or executed.[1] One developer reported spending 2 hours debugging when different file instances contained different content.[1]\n\n## File Corruption and Duplication\n\nBeyond multiple instances, Cursor has reported issues with file content duplication within the same file, where existing content is repeated unexpectedly.[2] There's also an issue where overlapping text from two files appears rendered in a single text window, making the overlapping content persist even when scrolling.[3]\n\n## Workarounds\n\nUsers experiencing these issues are advised to:[1]\n- Manually close all file instances\n- Use absolute file paths\n- Restart the editor\n\nThe issues appear to be bugs rather than intentional features, with users encouraged to report problems on the main bug report site at forum.cursor.com for faster responses.[3]",

{
  "markdown": "# Issue: specstoryai/getspecstory #196\n\n- Repository: specstoryai/getspecstory | Install our local first extensions for your favorite AI IDE or Terminal Agent. Sync your conversations to the cloud. File issues and requests. | 1K stars | Go\n\n## [EXTENSION/CLI] (BUG) Duplicate files per Cursor session with different content completeness; (FEATURE) Per-source filtering by agent in Extension\n\n- Author: [@guiguiluo](https://github.com/guiguiluo)\n- State: open\n- Labels: Bug, Feature request, Cursor Extension, SpecStory CLI\n- Created: 2026-03-20T07:57:35Z\n- Updated: 2026-03-20T13:29:55Z\n\n### Environment\n\n- Cursor version: latest (March 2026)\n- SpecStory extension: latest (Markdown v2.1.0, generating two header formats)\n- OS: Windows 10\n- Also using Codex CLI in same projects\n\n---\n\n## Bug 1: Every Cursor session generates two markdown files with different content\n\nEach Cursor conversation produces **two separate files** in &#96;.specstory/history/&#96;:\n\n| File A (no-seconds) | File B (with-seconds) |\n| --- | --- |\n| Filename | &#96;2026-03-09_11-11Z-title.md&#96; | &#96;2026-03-09_11-11-17Z-title.md&#96; |\n| Header comment | &#96; &#96; | &#96; &#96; |\n| Title format | &#96;# (readable title) (timestamp)&#96; | &#96;# 2026-03-09 11:11:17Z&#96; |\n| Message separators | &#96;---&#96; between turns | Each agent step gets its own &#96;_**Agent**_&#96; header |\n| Content completeness | **May truncate last messages** | **Complete** |\n\n**These two files are NOT identical.** File B (with seconds, &#96;cursoride&#96; tag) consistently has more complete content — the last agent responses in File A are often cut off mid-sentence. In one observed case, the delta was 4,863 lines.\n\nBoth files share the same underlying session UUID, confirming they represent the same conversation rendered twice.\n\n**Expected**: One file per session, with complete content.\n\n---\n\n## Bug 2: Sessions from Codex CLI and other tools are saved without opt-out, and many get meaningless names\n\n**Root cause**: SpecStory now captures conversations from Codex CLI, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, and other tools in addition to Cursor. However:\n\n1. **No per-source filter exists.** The only control is the global &#96;specstory.autoSave&#96; toggle. Users who only want Cursor history saved have no way to exclude other tools — it's all or nothing.\n\n1. **Codex CLI sessions generate large numbers of &#96;context-from-my-ide&#96; files.** When Codex starts a session, it automatically injects IDE context (open files, active file, etc.) as the first user message before the real request. SpecStory names the file from this injected content, resulting in dozens of files titled &#96;context-from-my-ide&#96; that are indistinguishable from each other and have no meaningful names. In my workspace, 11 out of 13 non-Cursor files had this name.\n\n1. **Sub-agent sessions are saved separately.** When Codex spawns a sub-agent for a specific task, that sub-agent's session is also captured as a separate file (e.g. &#96;layer-2-2-d.md&#96;), even though it's a programmatic subprocess, not a user-facing conversation.\n\nThe header comment in these files uses &#96;Codex CLI Session&#96; instead of &#96;cursor Session&#96;, so they are technically distinguishable — but only programmatically, not visually in the file browser.\n\n**Expected / requested**:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;jsonc\n// Per-source toggle settings\n\"specstory.autoSave.cursor\": true,\n\"specstory.autoSave.codex\": false,\n\"specstory.autoSave.claudeCode\": false,\n\"specstory.autoSave.gemini\": false,\n\n// Or alternatively, a skip rule for auto-context-injected sessions:\n\"specstory.skipContextOnlyMessages\": true\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n---\n\n### Timeline\n\n**@belucid** commented · Mar 20, 2026 at 12:56pm · edited\n\n\u003e ~~@guiguiluo can you tell me what version of the SpecStory CLI you are using? &#96;v1.12.0&#96; has a fix intended to address the &#96;context-from-my-ide&#96; files, and that fix is shared in &#96;v1.0.0&#96; of the Cursor/VSC extension.~~\n\u003e \n\u003e nm @guiguiluo I misread part of your note as saying you were also using the SpecStory CLI (standalone). You said you were also using the Codex CLI. Got it.\n\n**guiguiluo** was mentioned · Mar 20, 2026 at 12:56pm\n\n**belucid** added label &#96;Bug&#96; · Mar 20, 2026 at 12:57pm\n\n**belucid** added label &#96;Feature request&#96; · Mar 20, 2026 at 12:57pm\n\n**belucid** added label &#96;Cursor Extension&#96; · Mar 20, 2026 at 12:57pm\n\n**belucid** added label &#96;SpecStory CLI&#96; · Mar 20, 2026 at 12:57pm\n\n**belucid** changed the title from \"[EXTENSION/CLI] Two bugs: (1) Duplicate files per Cursor session with different content completeness; (2) No per-source filtering causes unwanted Codex/CLI sessions to pollute history\" to \"[EXTENSION/CLI] (Bug) Duplicate files per Cursor session with different content completeness; (FEATURE) No per-source filtering causes unwanted Codex/CLI sessions to pollute history\" · Mar 20, 2026 at 12:57pm\n\n**belucid** changed the title from \"[EXTENSION/CLI] (Bug) Duplicate files per Cursor session with different content completeness; (FEATURE) No per-source filtering causes unwanted Codex/CLI sessions to pollute history\" to \"[EXTENSION/CLI] (BUG) Duplicate files per Cursor session with different content completeness; (FEATURE) No per-source filtering causes unwanted Codex/CLI sessions to pollute history\" · Mar 20, 2026 at 12:57pm\n\n**belucid** changed the title from \"[EXTENSION/CLI] (BUG) Duplicate files per Cursor session with different content completeness; (FEATURE) No per-source filtering causes unwanted Codex/CLI sessions to pollute history\" to \"[EXTENSION/CLI] (BUG) Duplicate files per Cursor session with different content completeness; (FEATURE) Per-source filtering by agent in Extension\" · Mar 20, 2026 at 12:58pm\n\n**@bago2k4** commented · Mar 20, 2026 at 1:29pm\n\n\u003e Also @guiguiluo , some minor changes in the markdowns are expected since version 1.0.0. It will be a 1 time change and won't happen again.\n\u003e \n\u003e The extension logic changed substantially in this last version. 完美，现在全部信息到齐。以下是完整诊断。

---

## 一、SpecStory 双文件机制（已确认的 Bug）

GitHub issue [#196](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/issues/196) 明确记录了这个行为：**SpecStory 对同一段 Cursor 对话会在 `.specstory/history/` 里生成两个文件**：

| | File A（旧格式） | File B（新格式，带秒数） |
|---|---|---|
| 文件名 | `2026-03-17_06-00Z-复盘大脑op4-6.md` | `2026-03-17_06-00-54Z-复盘大脑op4.6.md` |
| 时间戳 | 不带秒（`HH-MMZ`） | 带秒（`HH-MM-SSZ`） |
| 标题 | 可读标题（`复盘大脑OP4.6`） | 纯时间戳（`2026-03-17 06:00:54Z`） |
| 标题 slug | 特殊字符被替换（`.` → `-`） | 保留原始字符（`.`） |
| 内容 | 有 `Cleaned from SpecStory` 标记，模型信息完整 | 缺模型信息，标题是时间戳 |
| 完整度 | 通常更完整 | 可能截断末尾 |

SpecStory 官方确认这是 v1.0.0 大改版引入的 bug，尚未修复。

---

## 二、WF1 当前去重逻辑的缺陷

WF1 的 `SpecStory预清洗` 节点有一个 `normalizeSpeName` 函数，试图把带秒和不带秒的文件名统一：

```31:31:e:\数字\N8N\01-A对话清洗标注.json
// 2026-03-17_06-00-54Z-xxx.md → 2026-03-17_06-00Z-xxx.md
function normalizeSpeName(name) {
  return name.replace(/(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}_\d{2}-\d{2})-\d{2}Z/, '$1Z');
}
```

**这个函数只解决了时间戳的差异，但没有解决标题 slug 的差异。** 同一对话的两个文件：

- `2026-03-17_06-00-54Z-复盘大脑op4.6.md` → normalize → `2026-03-17_06-00Z-复盘大脑op4.6.md`
- `2026-03-17_06-00Z-复盘大脑op4-6.md` → normalize → `2026-03-17_06-00Z-复盘大脑op4-6.md`

**`复盘大脑op4.6` ≠ `复盘大脑op4-6`**，去重失败，两个都进了管线。

同样的问题出现在所有包含特殊字符的标题上——SpecStory 的两个文件生成器对标题 slug 的处理规则不同（一个保留 `.`、`（）`，另一个替换为 `-`、`()`）。

---

## 三、还有一个问题：路径硬编码

WF1 所有节点都硬编码了 `D:\\数字分身2\\vault\\数字分身`，但你当前项目在 `E:\数字`。这意味着 WF1 目前在这台机器上根本跑不通——不过这是已知问题（备忘里标了），先聚焦去重。

---

## 四、修复方案

**核心思路**：既然同一对话的两个文件只有时间戳精度和标题 slug 不同，但「日期+时+分」部分一定相同，那么去重应该基于「分钟级时间戳」而不是完整文件名。

具体改法在 `SpecStory预清洗` 节点：

1. **读取 SPE 文件列表后，按分钟级时间戳分组**
2. **同一分钟内有多个文件时，只保留 File A（不带秒、内容更完整的那个）**
3. **已处理文件的去重比对也改为分钟级**

你确认这个方向后我直接改 JSON。另外需要确认：

- **路径问题**：要不要顺手把 `D:\\数字分身2\\vault\\数字分身` 改成 `E:\\数字\\vault\\数字分身`？
- **已有的重复待确认文件**：要不要写个脚本一次性清理掉 28 组重复中的冗余文件？

---

```json
{
  "markdown": "# agent-memory-skill\n\n[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)\n[![Python 3.10+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.10+-green.svg)](https://python.org)\n[![No Dependencies](https://img.shields.io/badge/Dependencies-Zero-brightgreen.svg)](#)\n\n**Memory system for AI agents based on the Ebbinghaus forgetting curve.**\n\nOne Python file. Zero dependencies. Works with any directory of markdown files.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n  Memory Strength\n  1.0 ┤████████████████████████████████████████████████  ← just accessed\n      │ ╲\n  0.9 ┤   ╲                                              ← Day 7: active\n      │     ╲\n  0.8 ┤       ╲\n      │         ╲\n  0.7 ┤           ╲                                       ← Day 21: warm\n      │             ╲\n  0.6 ┤               ╲\n      │                 ╲\n  0.5 ┤                   ╲                               ← Day 33: cold\n      │                     ╲\n  0.4 ┤                       ╲\n      │                         ╲\n  0.3 ┤                           ╲\n      │                             ╲\n  0.2 ┤                               ╲\n      │                                 ╲\n  0.1 ┤─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─ ─floor  ← Day 60+: archive\n      │\n  0.0 ┤\n      └──┬──────┬──────────────┬────────────┬──────────→  Days\n         0      7              21           60\n\n         active    warm          cold         archive\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## The Problem\n\nAI agents forget everything between sessions. Two common solutions — and why they don't work:\n\n**\"Dump everything into context\"** — Loads entire knowledge bases per turn. Burns 20K+ tokens before the conversation starts. No prioritization: a 2-month-old contact gets the same weight as yesterday's hot lead.\n\n**\"Vector search everything\"** — Retrieves by semantic similarity but has no temporal awareness. A card accessed yesterday and a card untouched for 90 days look identical. No concept of \"fading\" or \"forgetting.\" No serendipity.\n\nHuman memory does something different: it **decays over time**, **strengthens with use**, and occasionally **surprises you** with random connections. This skill brings that to AI agents.\n\n---\n\n## The Solution — 3-Layer Memory\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Layer 1: HOT CONTEXT              always loaded, \u003c4KB  │\n│  └── State file — active focus, blockers, reminders     │\n├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│  Layer 2: SEARCHABLE VAULT          on-demand, unlimited │\n│  └── Cards with YAML frontmatter — one file per entity  │\n│      Tiers: core → active → warm → cold → archive       │\n├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│  Layer 3: ARCHIVE                   deep/creative only   │\n│  └── Old logs, completed projects, cold contacts         │\n│      Still searchable, excluded from default queries     │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Rule:** Each fact lives in ONE place. If it's in a card, don't duplicate it in the state file.\n\nSee [docs/architecture.md](docs/architecture.md) for full design rationale.\n\n---\n\n## Forgetting Curve\n\nInspired by Hermann Ebbinghaus (1885). Each card has a &#96;relevance&#96; score (0.0–1.0) that decays linearly:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nrelevance = max(0.1, 1.0 - days × 0.015)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Tier Assignment\n\n| Tier | Days Since Access | Relevance | Behavior |\n|------|-------------------|-----------|----------|\n| **core** | manual | 1.0 | Never auto-demoted. Identity, security, pricing. |\n| **active** | 0–7 | 1.0–0.90 | Searched in all modes. Hot context. |\n| **warm** | 8–21 | 0.89–0.69 | Default search. Gradually fading. |\n| **cold** | 22–60 | 0.68–0.10 | Deep search only. Mostly forgotten. |\n| **archive** | 60+ | 0.10 (floor) | Creative mode or explicit recall. |\n\n### Graduated Touch (Spaced Repetition)\n\nUnlike a simple \"reset to top,\" &#96;touch&#96; promotes **one tier at a time**:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\narchive → cold → warm → active → active (refresh)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nMultiple reads = stronger memory. Natural spaced repetition without manual scheduling.\n\n---\n\n## Search Modes\n\n| Mode | Tiers Searched | When to Use | Token Cost |\n|------|---------------|-------------|------------|\n| **heartbeat** | core + active | Quick status checks, monitoring | ~2K |\n| **normal** | active + warm | Most questions, task execution | ~5K |\n| **deep** | all tiers | Strategy, \"find everything about X\" | ~15K |\n| **creative** | random cold+archive | Brainstorming, ideation, \"what if\" | ~3K |\n\n### Creative Mode — The Highlight Feature\n\nHuman creativity comes from random associations — shower thoughts, serendipitous encounters, dreams. Creative mode simulates this by pulling **random forgotten cards** back into working memory.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npython3 memory-engine.py creative 5 vault/\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n  creative recall — 5 random cards:\n    [cold] Cloud Migration Strategy\n           projects/cloud-migration.md (r=0.25, last=2026-01-05)\n    [archive] React Native Performance Notes\n           notes/rn-performance.md (r=0.1, last=2025-11-20)\n    ...\n\n  read these cards and look for unexpected connections to your current task\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe randomness is the feature, not a bug.\n\nSee [docs/search-protocols.md](docs/search-protocols.md) for detailed protocols.\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# 1. Copy to your project\ncurl -O https://raw.githubusercontent.com/smixs/agent-memory-skill/main/memory-engine.py\n\n# 2. Scan your vault\npython3 memory-engine.py scan vault/\n\n# 3. Initialize cards (add YAML frontmatter)\npython3 memory-engine.py init vault/ --dry-run   # preview first\npython3 memory-engine.py init vault/              # apply\n\n# 4. Run decay (update relevance scores)\npython3 memory-engine.py decay vault/\n\n# 5. Creative recall (surface forgotten cards)\npython3 memory-engine.py creative 5 vault/\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## All Commands\n\n| Command | Description | Example |\n|---------|-------------|---------|\n| &#96;scan&#96; | Analyze files, report stats (no changes) | &#96;memory-engine.py scan vault/&#96; |\n| &#96;init&#96; | Add YAML frontmatter to files missing it | &#96;memory-engine.py init vault/ --dry-run&#96; |\n| &#96;decay&#96; | Update relevance scores and tiers | &#96;memory-engine.py decay vault/&#96; |\n| &#96;touch&#96; | Promote card one tier up (graduated recall) | &#96;memory-engine.py touch vault/crm/acme.md&#96; |\n| &#96;creative&#96; | Random N cards from cold/archive tiers | &#96;memory-engine.py creative 5 vault/&#96; |\n| &#96;daily&#96; | Bootstrap and decay daily files (YYYY-MM-DD.md) | &#96;memory-engine.py daily vault/daily/&#96; |\n| &#96;stats&#96; | Show tier distribution and health metrics | &#96;memory-engine.py stats vault/&#96; |\n| &#96;config&#96; | Generate default &#96;.memory-config.json&#96; | &#96;memory-engine.py config vault/&#96; |\n\n### Global Options\n\n| Flag | Description |\n|------|-------------|\n| &#96;--dry-run&#96; | Preview changes without writing files |\n| &#96;--verbose&#96; | Show per-file details |\n| &#96;--config \u003cpath\u003e&#96; | Custom config file path |\n\n---\n\n## YAML Schema\n\n### Required Fields\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml\n---\ntype: crm                         # crm | lead | contact | project | personal | daily | note\ndescription: \u003e-                   # One-line search snippet\n  Cloud provider, enterprise tier\ntags: [cloud, enterprise]          # 2-5 freeform tags\nstatus: active                    # active | draft | pending | done | inactive\n---\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Auto-Managed Fields (by memory-engine.py)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml\nlast_accessed: 2026-02-20         # When card was last read/touched\nrelevance: 0.85                   # 0.0-1.0, decays over time\ntier: active                      # core | active | warm | cold | archive\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Full Example\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml\n---\ntype: crm\ndescription: \u003e-\n  Cloud infrastructure provider, enterprise tier, renewal Q2 2026\ntags: [cloud, enterprise, renewal]\nstatus: active\nindustry: IT\nregion: US\ncreated: 2026-01-15\nupdated: 2026-02-20\nlast_accessed: 2026-02-20\nrelevance: 0.85\ntier: active\n---\n\n# Acme Cloud Corp\n\n## Overview\n- **Industry:** Cloud Infrastructure\n- **Contact:** Jane Smith, VP Sales\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSee [docs/yaml-schema.md](docs/yaml-schema.md) for the full schema reference.\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\nGenerate a default config:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npython3 memory-engine.py config vault/\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nCreates &#96;.memory-config.json&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"tiers\": {\"active\": 7, \"warm\": 21, \"cold\": 60},\n  \"decay_rate\": 0.015,\n  \"relevance_floor\": 0.1,\n  \"skip_patterns\": [\"_index.md\"],\n  \"type_inference\": {\"crm/\": \"crm\", \"leads/\": \"lead\"},\n  \"use_git_dates\": true\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Domain-Specific Tuning\n\n| Domain | Active | Warm | Cold | Rate | Why |\n|--------|--------|------|------|------|-----|\n| Sales/CRM | 3 | 10 | 30 | 0.025 | Fast-moving, deals expire quickly |\n| Default | 7 | 21 | 60 | 0.015 | Balanced for general use |\n| Research | 14 | 45 | 120 | 0.008 | Knowledge stays relevant longer |\n\n### Config Fields\n\n| Field | Type | Default | Description |\n|-------|------|---------|-------------|\n| &#96;tiers.active&#96; | int | 7 | Days threshold for active tier |\n| &#96;tiers.warm&#96; | int | 21 | Days threshold for warm tier |\n| &#96;tiers.cold&#96; | int | 60 | Days threshold for cold tier |\n| &#96;decay_rate&#96; | float | 0.015 | Relevance loss per day |\n| &#96;relevance_floor&#96; | float | 0.1 | Minimum relevance (never reaches 0) |\n| &#96;skip_patterns&#96; | list | &#96;[\"_index.md\"]&#96; | Glob patterns to skip |\n| &#96;type_inference&#96; | object | &#96;{}&#96; | Path → type mapping for auto-inference |\n| &#96;use_git_dates&#96; | bool | true | Use git log for date resolution |\n\n---\n\n## Integration with Claude Code\n\n### As a Skill\n\nCopy the files into your project's skill directory:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n.claude/skills/agent-memory/\n├── SKILL.md              # Skill definition (Claude reads this)\n├── scripts/\n│   └── memory-engine.py  # Engine\n└── references/\n    ├── architecture.md\n    ├── search-protocols.md\n    └── yaml-schema.md\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nClaude Code will automatically discover and use the skill based on &#96;SKILL.md&#96;.\n\n### Daily Decay via Cron\n\nAdd to your crontab for automatic forgetting:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Run decay every day at midnight\n0 0 * * * cd /path/to/vault \u0026\u0026 python3 memory-engine.py decay .\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOr via systemd timer for more reliability.\n\n### Full System\n\nFor the complete voice-first AI assistant (Telegram → Obsidian + Todoist), see [agent-second-brain](https://github.com/smixs/agent-second-brain).\n\n---\n\n## How It Compares\n\n| | Dump All | Vector DB | **agent-memory** |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **Context cost** | High (all cards every turn) | Medium (top-K results) | Low (tiered loading) |\n| **Temporal awareness** | None | None | Built-in (decay + tiers) |\n| **Serendipity** | None | None | Creative mode |\n| **Dependencies** | None | Embedding model + DB | None (pure Python) |\n| **Setup** | Copy files | Deploy infra | Copy one file |\n| **Spaced repetition** | No | No | Graduated touch |\n| **Works offline** | Yes | Needs model API | Yes |\n\n---\n\n## Anti-Patterns\n\n| Don't | Do Instead |\n|-------|------------|\n| Load all cards into context | Search on demand, filter by tier |\n| Store same fact in 3 files | One card per entity, reference via links |\n| Delete old files to \"save space\" | Let tier decay handle visibility |\n| Touch every card during bulk ops | Only touch on meaningful read/update |\n| Build elaborate review systems | Let decay handle it — unused = fades |\n\n---\n\n## License\n\n[MIT](LICENSE)",
  "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/smixs/agent-memory-skill/main/README.md"
}
```

{
  "markdown": "# Claudeception\n\nEvery time you use an AI coding agent, it starts from zero. You spend an hour debugging some obscure error, the agent figures it out, session ends. Next time you hit the same issue? Another hour.\n\nThis skill fixes that. When Claude Code discovers something non-obvious (a debugging technique, a workaround, some project-specific pattern), it saves that knowledge as a new skill. Next time a similar problem comes up, the skill gets loaded automatically.\n\n## Installation\n\n### Step 1: Clone the skill\n\n**User-level (recommended)**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ngit clone https://github.com/blader/Claudeception.git ~/.claude/skills/claudeception\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n**Project-level**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ngit clone https://github.com/blader/Claudeception.git .claude/skills/claudeception\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 2: Set up the activation hook (recommended)\n\nThe skill can activate via semantic matching, but a hook ensures it evaluates every session for extractable knowledge.\n\n#### User-level setup (recommended)\n\n1. Create the hooks directory and copy the script:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nmkdir -p ~/.claude/hooks\ncp ~/.claude/skills/claudeception/scripts/claudeception-activator.sh ~/.claude/hooks/\nchmod +x ~/.claude/hooks/claudeception-activator.sh\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n2. Add the hook to your global Claude settings (&#96;~/.claude/settings.json&#96;):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"UserPromptSubmit\": [\n      {\n        \"hooks\": [\n          {\n            \"type\": \"command\",\n            \"command\": \"~/.claude/hooks/claudeception-activator.sh\"\n          }\n        ]\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n#### Project-level setup\n\n1. Create the hooks directory inside your project and copy the script:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nmkdir -p .claude/hooks\ncp .claude/skills/claudeception/scripts/claudeception-activator.sh .claude/hooks/\nchmod +x .claude/hooks/claudeception-activator.sh\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n2. Add the hook to your project settings (&#96;.claude/settings.json&#96; in the repo):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"hooks\": {\n    \"UserPromptSubmit\": [\n      {\n        \"hooks\": [\n          {\n            \"type\": \"command\",\n            \"command\": \".claude/hooks/claudeception-activator.sh\"\n          }\n        ]\n      }\n    ]\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nIf you already have a &#96;settings.json&#96;, merge the &#96;hooks&#96; configuration into it.\n\nThe hook injects a reminder on every prompt that tells Claude to evaluate whether the current task produced extractable knowledge. This achieves higher activation rates than relying on semantic description matching alone.\n\n## Usage\n\n### Automatic Mode\n\nThe skill activates automatically when Claude Code:\n- Just completed debugging and discovered a non-obvious solution\n- Found a workaround through investigation or trial-and-error\n- Resolved an error where the root cause wasn't immediately apparent\n- Learned project-specific patterns or configurations through investigation\n- Completed any task where the solution required meaningful discovery\n\n### Explicit Mode\n\nTrigger a learning retrospective:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n/claudeception\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOr explicitly request skill extraction:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSave what we just learned as a skill\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### What Gets Extracted\n\nNot every task produces a skill. It only extracts knowledge that required actual discovery (not just reading docs), will help with future tasks, has clear trigger conditions, and has been verified to work.\n\n## Research\n\nThe idea comes from academic work on skill libraries for AI agents.\n\n[Voyager](https://arxiv.org/abs/2305.16291) (Wang et al., 2023) showed that game-playing agents can build up libraries of reusable skills over time, and that this helps them avoid re-learning things they already figured out. [CASCADE](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.23880) (2024) introduced \"meta-skills\" (skills for acquiring skills), which is what this is. [SEAgent](https://arxiv.org/abs/2508.04700) (2025) showed agents can learn new software environments through trial and error, which inspired the retrospective feature. [Reflexion](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366) (Shinn et al., 2023) showed that self-reflection helps.\n\nAgents that persist what they learn do better than agents that start fresh.\n\n## How It Works\n\nClaude Code has a native skills system. At startup, it loads skill names and descriptions (about 100 tokens each). When you're working, it matches your current context against those descriptions and pulls in relevant skills.\n\nBut this retrieval system can be written to, not just read from. So when this skill notices extractable knowledge, it writes a new skill with a description optimized for future retrieval.\n\nThe description matters a lot. \"Helps with database problems\" won't match anything useful. \"Fix for PrismaClientKnownRequestError in serverless\" will match when someone hits that error.\n\nMore on the skills architecture [here](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/equipping-agents-for-the-real-world-with-agent-skills).\n\n## Skill Format\n\nExtracted skills are markdown files with YAML frontmatter:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;yaml\n---\nname: prisma-connection-pool-exhaustion\ndescription: |\n  Fix for PrismaClientKnownRequestError: Too many database connections \n  in serverless environments (Vercel, AWS Lambda). Use when connection \n  count errors appear after ~5 concurrent requests.\nauthor: Claude Code\nversion: 1.0.0\ndate: 2024-01-15\n---\n\n# Prisma Connection Pool Exhaustion\n\n## Problem\n[What this skill solves]\n\n## Context / Trigger Conditions\n[Exact error messages, symptoms, scenarios]\n\n## Solution\n[Step-by-step fix]\n\n## Verification\n[How to confirm it worked]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSee &#96;resources/skill-template.md&#96; for the full template.\n\n## Quality Gates\n\nThe skill is picky about what it extracts. If something is just a documentation lookup, or only useful for this one case, or hasn't actually been tested, it won't create a skill. Would this actually help someone who hits this problem in six months? If not, no skill.\n\n## Examples\n\nSee &#96;examples/&#96; for sample skills:\n\n- &#96;nextjs-server-side-error-debugging/&#96;: errors that don't show in browser console\n- &#96;prisma-connection-pool-exhaustion/&#96;: the \"too many connections\" serverless problem\n- &#96;typescript-circular-dependency/&#96;: detecting and fixing import cycles\n\n## Contributing\n\nContributions welcome. Fork, make changes, submit a PR.\n\n## License\n\nMIT",
  "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/blader/Claudeception/main/README.md"
}
```

{
  "markdown": "\u003cdiv align=\"center\"\u003e\n  \u003ca href=\"https://discord.acontext.io\"\u003e\n      \u003cimg alt=\"Acontext - Agent Skills as a Memory Layer\" src=\"./assets/Acontext-header-banner.png\"\u003e\n  \u003c/a\u003e\n \t\u003cp align=\"center\"\u003e\n \t  \t\u003ca href=\"https://acontext.io\"\u003e🌐 Website\u003c/a\u003e\n      |\n \t  \t\u003ca href=\"https://docs.acontext.io\"\u003e📚 Document\u003c/a\u003e\n  \u003c/p\u003e\n  \u003cp align=\"center\"\u003e\n    \u003ca href=\"https://pypi.org/project/acontext/\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/acontext.svg\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\n    \u003ca href=\"https://www.npmjs.com/package/@acontext/acontext\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@acontext/acontext.svg?logo=npm\u0026logoColor=fff\u0026style=flat\u0026labelColor=2C2C2C\u0026color=28CF8D\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\n    \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/memodb-io/acontext/actions/workflows/core-test.yaml\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://github.com/memodb-io/acontext/actions/workflows/core-test.yaml/badge.svg\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\n    \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/memodb-io/acontext/actions/workflows/api-test.yaml\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://github.com/memodb-io/acontext/actions/workflows/api-test.yaml/badge.svg\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\n    \u003ca href=\"https://github.com/memodb-io/acontext/actions/workflows/cli-test.yaml\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://github.com/memodb-io/acontext/actions/workflows/cli-test.yaml/badge.svg\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\n  \u003c/p\u003e\n\u003cp align=\"center\"\u003e\n \t  \t\u003ca href=\"https://x.com/acontext_io\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://img.shields.io/twitter/follow/acontext_io?style=social\" alt=\"Twitter Follow\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\n    \u003ca href=\"https://discord.acontext.io\"\u003e\u003cimg src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/dynamic/json?label=Acontext\u0026style=flat\u0026query=approximate_member_count\u0026url=https%3A%2F%2Fdiscord.com%2Fapi%2Fv10%2Finvites%2FSG9xJcqVBu%3Fwith_counts%3Dtrue\u0026logo=discord\u0026logoColor=white\u0026suffix=+members\u0026color=36393f\u0026labelColor=5765F2\" alt=\"Acontext Discord\"\u003e\u003c/a\u003e\n  \u003c/p\u003e\n\u003c/div\u003e\n\n\n## What is Acontext?\n\nAcontext is an open-source skill memory layer for AI agents. It **automatically** captures learnings from agent runs and stores them as **agent skill files** — files you can read, edit, and share across agents, LLMs, and frameworks.\n\nIf you want the agent you build to **learn from its mistakes** and **reuse what worked** — without opaque memory polluting your context — give Acontext a try.\n\n\n## Skill is All You Need\n\nAgent memory is getting increasingly complicated🤢 — hard to understand, hard to debug, and hard for users to inspect or correct. Acontext takes a different approach: if agent skills can represent every piece of knowledge an agent needs as simple files, so can the memory.\n\n- **Acontext builds memory in the agent skills format**, so everyone can see and understand what the memory actually contains.\n- **Skill is Memory, Memory is Skill**. Whether a skill comes from one you downloaded from Clawhub or one you created yourself, Acontext can follow it and evolve it over time.\n\n\n## The Philosophy of Acontext\n\n- **Plain file, any framework** — Skill memories are Markdown files. Use them with LangGraph, Claude, AI SDK, or anything that reads files. No embeddings, no API lock-in. Git, grep, and mount to the sandbox.\n- **You design the structure** — Attach more skills to define the schema, naming, and file layout of the memory. For example: one file per contact, one per project by uploading a working context skill.\n- **Progressive disclosure, not search** — The agent can use  &#96;get_skill&#96; and &#96;get_skill_file&#96; to fetch what it needs. Retrieval is by tool use and reasoning, not semantic top-k.\n- **Download as ZIP, reuse anywhere** — Export skill files as ZIP. Run locally, in another agent, or with another LLM. No vendor lock-in; no re-embedding or migration step.\n\n## How It Works\n\n### Store — How skills get memorized?\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  A[Session messages] --\u003e C[Task complete/failed]\n  C --\u003e D[Distillation]\n  D --\u003e E[Skill Agent]\n  E --\u003e F[Update Skills]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- **Session messages** — Conversation (and optionally tool calls, artifacts) is the raw input. Tasks are extracted from the message stream automatically (or inferred from explicit outcome reporting).\n- **Task complete or failed** — When a task is marked done or failed (e.g. by agent report or automatic detection), that outcome is the trigger for learning.\n- **Distillation** — An LLM pass infers from the conversation and execution trace what worked, what failed, and user preferences.\n- **Skill Agent** — Decides where to store (existing skill or new) and writes according to your &#96;SKILL.md&#96; schema.\n- **Update Skills** — Skills are updated. You define the structure in &#96;SKILL.md&#96;; the system does extraction, routing, and writing.\n\n### Recall — How the agent uses skills on the next run\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  E[Any Agent] --\u003e F[list_skills/get_skill]\n  F --\u003e G[Appear in context]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nGive your agent **Skill Content Tools** (&#96;get_skill&#96;, &#96;get_skill_file&#96;). The agent decides what it needs, calls the tools, and gets the skill content. No embedding search — **progressive disclosure, agent in the loop**.\n\n\n# 🪜 Use It to Improve your Agent\n\nClaude Code: \n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\nRead https://acontext.io/SKILL.md and follow the instructions to install and configure Acontext for Claude Code\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOpenClaw:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;text\nRead https://acontext.io/SKILL.md and follow the instructions to install and configure Acontext for OpenClaw\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\n# 🚀 Step-by-step Quickstart\n\n### Connect to Acontext\n\n1. Go to [Acontext.io](https://acontext.io), claim your free credits.\n2. Go through a one-click onboarding to get your API Key (starts with &#96;sk-ac&#96;)\n\n\u003cdiv align=\"center\"\u003e\n    \u003cpicture\u003e\n      \u003cimg alt=\"Dashboard\" src=\"./assets/onboard.png\" width=\"80%\"\u003e\n    \u003c/picture\u003e\n\u003c/div\u003e\n\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e💻 Self-host Acontext\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nWe have an &#96;acontext-cli&#96; to help you do a quick proof-of-concept. Download it first in your terminal:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ncurl -fsSL https://install.acontext.io | sh\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou should have [docker](https://www.docker.com/get-started/) installed and an OpenAI API Key to start an Acontext backend on your computer:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nmkdir acontext_server \u0026\u0026 cd acontext_server\nacontext server up\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\u003e Make sure your LLM has the ability to [call tools](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling). By default, Acontext will use &#96;gpt-4.1&#96;.\n\n&#96;acontext server up&#96; will create/use &#96;.env&#96; and &#96;config.yaml&#96; for Acontext, and create a &#96;db&#96; folder to persist data.\n\n\nOnce it's done, you can access the following endpoints:\n\n- Acontext API Base URL: http://localhost:8029/api/v1\n- Acontext Dashboard: http://localhost:3000/\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\n### Install SDKs\n\nWe're maintaining Python [![pypi](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/acontext.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/acontext/) and Typescript [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@acontext/acontext.svg?logo=npm\u0026logoColor=fff\u0026style=flat\u0026labelColor=2C2C2C\u0026color=28CF8D)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@acontext/acontext) SDKs. The snippets below are using Python.\n\n\u003e Click the doc link to see TS SDK Quickstart.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npip install acontext\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\n### Initialize Client\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nimport os\nfrom acontext import AcontextClient\n\n# For cloud:\nclient = AcontextClient(\n    api_key=os.getenv(\"ACONTEXT_API_KEY\"),\n)\n\n# For self-hosted:\nclient = AcontextClient(\n    base_url=\"http://localhost:8029/api/v1\",\n    api_key=\"sk-ac-your-root-api-bearer-token\",\n)\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\n### Skill Memory in Action\n\nCreate a learning space, attach a session, and let the agent learn — skills are written as Markdown files automatically.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;python\nfrom acontext import AcontextClient\n\nclient = AcontextClient(api_key=\"sk-ac-...\")\n\n# Create a learning space and attach a session\nspace = client.learning_spaces.create()\nsession = client.sessions.create()\nclient.learning_spaces.learn(space.id, session_id=session.id)\n\n# Run your agent, store messages — when tasks complete, learning runs automatically\nclient.sessions.store_message(session.id, blob={\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"My name is Gus\"})\nclient.sessions.store_message(session.id, blob={\"role\": \"assistant\", \"content\": \"Hi Gus! How can I help you today?\"})\n# ... agent runs ...\n\n# List learned skills (Markdown files)\nclient.learning_spaces.wait_for_learning(space.id, session_id=session.id)\nskills = client.learning_spaces.list_skills(space.id)\n\n# Download all skill files to a local directory\nfor skill in skills:\n    client.skills.download(skill_id=skill.id, path=f\"./skills/{skill.name}\")\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n\u003e &#96;wait_for_learning&#96; is a blocking helper for demo purposes. In production, task extraction and learning run in the background automatically — your agent never waits.\n\n### More Features\n\n- **[Context Engineering](https://docs.acontext.io/engineering/editing)** — Compress context with summaries and edit strategies\n- **[Disk](https://docs.acontext.io/store/disk)** — Virtual, persistent filesystem for agents\n- **[Sandbox](https://docs.acontext.io/store/sandbox)** — Isolated code execution with bash, Python, and [mountable skills](https://docs.acontext.io/tool/bash_tools#mounting-skills-in-sandbox)\n- **[Agent Tools](https://docs.acontext.io/tool/whatis)** — Disk tools, sandbox tools, and skill tools for LLM function calling\n\n\n# 🧐 Use Acontext to Build Agents\n\nDownload end-to-end scripts with &#96;acontext&#96;:\n\n**Python**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nacontext create my-proj --template-path \"python/openai-basic\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nMore examples on Python:\n\n- &#96;python/openai-agent-basic&#96;: openai agent sdk template\n- &#96;python/openai-agent-artifacts&#96;: agent can edit and download artifacts\n- &#96;python/claude-agent-sdk&#96;: claude agent sdk with &#96;ClaudeAgentStorage&#96;\n- &#96;python/agno-basic&#96;: agno framework template\n- &#96;python/smolagents-basic&#96;: smolagents (huggingface) template\n- &#96;python/interactive-agent-skill&#96;: interactive sandbox with mountable agent skills\n\n**Typescript**\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nacontext create my-proj --template-path \"typescript/openai-basic\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nMore examples on Typescript:\n- &#96;typescript/vercel-ai-basic&#96;: agent in @vercel/ai-sdk\n- &#96;typescript/claude-agent-sdk&#96;: claude agent sdk with &#96;ClaudeAgentStorage&#96;\n- &#96;typescript/interactive-agent-skill&#96;: interactive sandbox with mountable agent skills\n\n\n\u003e [!NOTE]\n\u003e\n\u003e Check our example repo for more templates: [Acontext-Examples](https://github.com/memodb-io/Acontext-Examples).\n\u003e\n\u003e We're cooking more full-stack Agent Applications! [Tell us what you want!](https://discord.acontext.io)\n\n\n# 🔍 Documentation\n\nTo learn more about skill memory and what Acontext can do, visit [our docs](https://docs.acontext.io/) or start with [What is Skill Memory?](https://docs.acontext.io/learn/quick)\n\n\n# ❤️ Stay Updated\n\nStar Acontext on GitHub to support us and receive instant _**Agent (claude-4.6-opus-max)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="web_fetch">

```json
{
  "markdown": "# Claude Code Semantic Memory System\n\nA persistent memory system for Claude Code that extracts learnings from past sessions and injects relevant context on every prompt.\n\n## The Problem\n\nClaude Code sessions are stateless by default. Every time context compacts or you start a new session, Claude forgets:\n- Solutions you already discovered together\n- Gotchas and traps you identified\n- Your infrastructure details and preferences\n- Decisions you made and why\n\nThis leads to repeated mistakes, redundant conversations, and lost productivity.\n\n## The Solution\n\nThis system gives Claude **persistent memory** across sessions:\n\n1. **Convert** your &#96;.jsonl&#96; transcripts to readable markdown\n2. **Extract** learnings using Claude sub-agents that process transcripts\n3. **Embed** learnings with a local embedding model (nomic-embed-text)\n4. **Inject** relevant memories via Claude Code hooks that fire on every prompt\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐\n│  Your Prompt    │────►│  Hook Fires     │────►│  Query Daemon   │\n│                 │     │  (mechanical)   │     │  (cosine sim)   │\n└─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘     └────────┬────────┘\n                                                         │\n                                                         ▼\n┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐     ┌─────────────────┐\n│  Claude sees    │◄────│  Inject as XML  │◄────│  Top 3 memories │\n│  context + mem  │     │  in context     │     │  (≥0.45 sim)    │\n└─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘     └─────────────────┘\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Quick Start\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n- [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/) (for transcript conversion)\n- [Ollama](https://ollama.com/) (for local embeddings)\n- Python 3.8+ (for the memory daemon)\n- Claude Code CLI\n\n### 1. Install Dependencies\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Install Ollama\ncurl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh\n\n# Pull the embedding model\nollama pull nomic-embed-text\n\n# Clone this repo\ngit clone https://github.com/zacdcook/claude-code-semantic-memory.git\ncd claude-code-semantic-memory\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 2. Convert Your Transcripts\n\nClaude Code stores session transcripts as &#96;.jsonl&#96; files in &#96;~/.claude/projects/&#96;. Convert them to readable markdown:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nnode scripts/jsonl-to-markdown.js ~/.claude/projects/ ./converted-transcripts/\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThis extracts user messages, assistant messages (including thinking blocks), and system prompts. Tool calls and results are stripped for cleaner extraction.\n\n### 3. Extract Learnings\n\nStart a new Claude Code session and use the extraction prompt:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nclaude\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen paste the contents of &#96;prompts/extract-learnings.md&#96;. Claude will:\n\n1. List all &#96;.md&#96; files in your converted transcripts folder\n2. Dispatch sub-agents in parallel to process batches\n3. Each sub-agent extracts structured learnings\n4. Store learnings via the daemon's &#96;/store&#96; endpoint\n5. Output to &#96;~/extracted-learnings.jsonl&#96;\n\n### 4. Start the Memory Daemon\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\ncd daemon\npip install -r requirements.txt\npython server.py\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe daemon runs on port 8741 and provides:\n- &#96;POST /store&#96; - Embed and store a learning\n- &#96;POST /recall&#96; - Query for relevant memories\n- &#96;GET /health&#96; - Health check\n\n### 5. Import Your Learnings\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npython scripts/import-learnings.py ~/extracted-learnings.jsonl\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 6. Install the Hooks\n\nCopy all hooks to your Claude Code hooks directory:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Session initialization\ncp hooks/session-start.sh ~/.claude/hooks/SessionStart.sh\n\n# Memory injection on prompts\ncp hooks/user-prompt-submit.sh ~/.claude/hooks/UserPromptSubmit.sh\n\n# Memory injection during iteration\ncp hooks/pre-tool-use.sh ~/.claude/hooks/PreToolUse.sh\n\n# Auto-export on compaction\ncp hooks/pre-compact.sh ~/.claude/hooks/PreCompact.sh\n\n# Make executable\nchmod +x ~/.claude/hooks/*.sh\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nNow:\n- Every prompt automatically queries memory and injects relevant learnings\n- During iteration, Claude's thinking is analyzed for additional relevant memories\n- When context compacts, the transcript is exported and a sub-agent extracts learnings\n\n---\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Hook Lifecycle\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nSESSION START\n════════════\n┌─────────────────┐\n│  SessionStart   │ → Check daemon health\n│                 │ → Warn about orphaned transcripts\n└────────┬────────┘\n         │\nACTIVE WORK (repeats for each user message)\n════════════\n         ▼\n┌─────────────────┐\n│UserPromptSubmit │ → Embed user's prompt\n│                 │ → Query daemon /recall\n│                 │ → Inject top 3 memories\n└────────┬────────┘\n         │\n         ▼  (fires before EACH tool)\n┌─────────────────┐\n│  PreToolUse     │ → Extract Claude's thinking\n│                 │ → Query for new relevant memories\n│                 │ → Inject if thinking has drifted\n└────────┬────────┘\n         │\nCONTEXT COMPACTION (when context window fills)\n════════════════════\n         ▼\n┌─────────────────┐\n│  PreCompact     │ → Export transcript to disk\n│                 │ → Convert JSONL to markdown\n│                 │ → Output sub-agent dispatch instructions\n└────────┬────────┘\n         │\n         ▼\n┌─────────────────┐\n│  Sub-Agent      │ → Read exported transcript\n│  (Task tool)    │ → Extract learnings\n│                 │ → Store via daemon /store endpoint\n└─────────────────┘\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Sub-Agent Extraction on Compaction\n\nWhen context compacts, the PreCompact hook exports the transcript and outputs instructions for Claude to dispatch a sub-agent. The sub-agent:\n\n1. Reads the exported markdown transcript\n2. Extracts learnings (solutions, gotchas, patterns, etc.)\n3. Stores each learning via the daemon's &#96;/store&#96; endpoint\n\nThis keeps everything within Claude Code - no external API calls needed. The sub-agent uses the Task tool to run without blocking.\n\n### Learning Types\n\n| Type | Description | Example |\n|------|-------------|---------|\n| &#96;WORKING_SOLUTION&#96; | Confirmed working commands/patterns | \"Use &#96;Import-Clixml&#96; for PowerShell credentials over Tailscale\" |\n| &#96;GOTCHA&#96; | Traps and counterintuitive behaviors | \"Git Bash strips &#96;$&#96; variables before PowerShell sees them\" |\n| &#96;PATTERN&#96; | Recurring architectural decisions | \"Check HOSTNAME in hooks to determine daemon URL\" |\n| &#96;DECISION&#96; | Explicit design choices with reasoning | \"Using nomic-embed-text for 8K token context\" |\n| &#96;FAILURE&#96; | What didn't work and why | \"SSH-based PS remoting fails due to TTY requirements\" |\n| &#96;PREFERENCE&#96; | User's stated preferences | \"Query memory before asking clarifying questions\" |\n\n### Why These Design Choices\n\n| Decision | Why |\n|----------|-----|\n| **Hooks over CLAUDE.md** | Hooks fire deterministically; CLAUDE.md instructions are suggestions Claude may skip under cognitive load |\n| **nomic-embed-text over MiniLM** | 8K token context vs 256 tokens — MiniLM truncates 75% of longer conversation turns |\n| **0.45 similarity threshold** | Permissive enough to catch semantically related content, not so low it floods with noise |\n| **Sub-agent extraction** | Uses Claude Code's own capabilities; no external API keys or local LLMs needed |\n\n### Database Schema\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;sql\nCREATE TABLE learnings (\n    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,\n    type TEXT NOT NULL,\n    content TEXT NOT NULL,\n    context TEXT,\n    embedding BLOB NOT NULL,\n    confidence REAL DEFAULT 0.9,\n    session_source TEXT,\n    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP\n);\n\nCREATE INDEX idx_learnings_type ON learnings(type);\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## File Structure\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nclaude-code-semantic-memory/\n├── README.md\n├── scripts/\n│   ├── jsonl-to-markdown.js    # Convert transcripts\n│   └── import-learnings.py     # Import JSONL to database\n├── prompts/\n│   └── extract-learnings.md    # Prompt for sub-agent extraction\n├── daemon/\n│   ├── server.py               # Flask API server\n│   ├── requirements.txt\n│   └── config.json             # Similarity thresholds, model config\n├── hooks/\n│   ├── session-start.sh        # Check daemon, warn orphans\n│   ├── user-prompt-submit.sh   # Memory injection on prompts\n│   ├── pre-tool-use.sh         # Memory injection during iteration\n│   └── pre-compact.sh          # Auto-export and dispatch sub-agent\n└── examples/\n    └── sample-learnings.jsonl  # Example output format\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## Configuration\n\nEdit &#96;daemon/config.json&#96;:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"embeddingModel\": \"nomic-embed-text\",\n  \"minSimilarity\": 0.45,\n  \"maxResults\": 3,\n  \"duplicateThreshold\": 0.92,\n  \"timeoutMs\": 2500,\n  \"port\": 8741\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n| Parameter | Default | Description |\n|-----------|---------|-------------|\n| &#96;embeddingModel&#96; | &#96;nomic-embed-text&#96; | Ollama model for embeddings (768 dimensions) |\n| &#96;minSimilarity&#96; | &#96;0.45&#96; | Minimum cosine similarity to return a memory |\n| &#96;maxResults&#96; | &#96;3&#96; | Maximum memories to inject per query |\n| &#96;duplicateThreshold&#96; | &#96;0.92&#96; | Similarity threshold for deduplication |\n| &#96;timeoutMs&#96; | &#96;2500&#96; | Max time to wait for embedding |\n| &#96;port&#96; | &#96;8741&#96; | Daemon port |\n\n---\n\n## Advanced Usage\n\n### Multi-Machine Setup\n\nIf you run Claude Code on a laptop but want embeddings on a GPU desktop:\n\n1. Run the daemon on your desktop\n2. Connect both machines via Tailscale (or any VPN)\n3. Set &#96;CLAUDE_DAEMON_HOST&#96; environment variable on your laptop:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nexport CLAUDE_DAEMON_HOST=100.95.72.101  # Desktop's Tailscale IP\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe hook will query the remote daemon instead of localhost.\n\n---\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n### Hook not firing\n- Check hook is executable: &#96;chmod +x ~/.claude/hooks/*.sh&#96;\n- Verify daemon is running: &#96;curl http://localhost:8741/health&#96;\n\n### No memories returned\n- Check similarity threshold isn't too high\n- Verify learnings were imported: &#96;curl http://localhost:8741/stats&#96;\n- Try a more specific query\n\n### Slow embedding\n- Ensure Ollama is using GPU: &#96;ollama ps&#96; should show CUDA\n- Reduce batch sizes if running out of memory\n\n---\n\n## Credits\n\n- [Ollama](https://ollama.com/) — Local embedding infrastructure\n- [nomic-embed-text](https://huggingface.co/nomic-ai/nomic-embed-text-v1.5) — Embedding model with 8K context\n\n---\n\n## License\n\nMIT",
  "url": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zacdcook/claude-code-semantic-memory/main/README.md"
}
```

Code\n\n[![Stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/affaan-m/everything-claude-code?style=flat)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/stargazers)\n[![Forks](https://img.shields.io/github/forks/affaan-m/everything-claude-code?style=flat)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/network/members)\n[![Contributors](https://img.shields.io/github/contributors/affaan-m/everything-claude-code?style=flat)](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/graphs/contributors)\n[![npm Dil**\n\n[**English**](README.md) | [Português (Brasil)](docs/pt-BR/README.md) | [简体中文](README.zh-CN.md) | [繁體中文](docs/zh-TW/README.md) | [日本語](docs/ja-JP/README.md) | [한국어](docs/ko-KR/README.md)\n | [Türkçe](docs/tr/README.md)\n\n\u003c/div\u003e\n\n---\n\n**The performance optimization system for AI agent harnesses. From an Anthropic hackathon winner.**\n\nNot just configs. A complete system: skills, instincts, memory optimization, continuous learning, security scanning, and research-first development. Production-ready agents, hooks, commands, rules, and MCP configurations evolved over 10+ months of intensive daily use building real products.\n\nWorks across **Claude Code**, **Codex**, **Cowork**, and other AI agent harnesses.\n\n---\n\n## The Guides\n\nThis repo is the raw code only. The guides explain everything.\n\n\u003ctable\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd width=\"33%\"\u003e\n\u003ca href=\"https://x.com/affaanmustafa/status/2012378465664745795\"\u003e\n\u003cimg src=\"./assets/images/guides/shorthand-guide.png\" alt=\"The Shorthand Guide to Everything Claude Code\" /\u003e\n\u003c/a\u003e\n\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd width=\"33%\"\u003e\n\u003ca href=\"https://x.com/affaanmustafa/status/2014040193557471352\"\u003e\n\u003cimg src=\"./assets/images/guides/longform-guide.png\" alt=\"The Longform Guide to Everything Claude Code\" /\u003e\n\u003c/a\u003e\n\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd width=\"33%\"\u003e\n\u003ca href=\"https://x.com/affaanmustafa/status/2033263813387223421\"\u003e\n\u003cimg src=\"./assets/images/security/security-guide-header.png\" alt=\"The Shorthand Guide to Everything Agentic Security\" /\u003e\n\u003c/a\u003e\n\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003ctr\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eShorthand Guide\u003c/b\u003e\u003cbr/\u003eSetup, foundations, philosophy. \u003cb\u003eRead this first.\u003c/b\u003e\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eLongform Guide\u003c/b\u003e\u003cbr/\u003eToken optimization, memory persistence, evals, parallelization.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003ctd align=\"center\"\u003e\u003cb\u003eSecurity Guide\u003c/b\u003e\u003cbr/\u003eAttack vectors, sandboxing, sanitization, CVEs, AgentShield.\u003c/td\u003e\n\u003c/tr\u003e\n\u003c/table\u003e\n\n| Topic | What You'll Learn |\n|-------|-------------------|\n| Token Optimization | Model selection, system prompt slimming, background processes |\n| Memory Persistence | Hooks that save/load context across sessions automatically |\n| Continuous Learning | Auto-extract patterns from sessions into reusable skills |\n| Verification Loops | Checkpoint vs continuous evals, grader types, pass@k metrics |\n| Parallelization | Git worktrees, cascade method, when to scale instances |\n| Subagent Orchestration | The context problem, iterative retrieval pattern |\n\n---\n\n## What's New\n\n### v1.9.0 — Selective Install \u0026 Language Expansion (Mar 2026)\n\n- **Selective install architecture** — Manifest-driven install pipeline with &#96;install-plan.js&#96; and &#96;install-apply.js&#96; for targeted component installation. State store tracks what's installed and enables incremental updates.\n- **6 new agents** — &#96;typescript-reviewer&#96;, &#96;pytorch-build-resolver&#96;, &#96;java-build-resolver&#96;, &#96;java-reviewer&#96;, &#96;kotlin-reviewer&#96;, &#96;kotlin-build-resolver&#96; expand language coverage to 10 languages.\n- **New skills** — &#96;pytorch-patterns&#96; for deep learning workflows, &#96;documentation-lookup&#96; for API reference research, &#96;bun-runtime&#96; and &#96;nextjs-turbopack&#96; for modern JS toolchains, plus 8 operational domain skills and &#96;mcp-server-patterns&#96;.\n- **Session \u0026 state infrastructure** — SQLite state store with query CLI, session adapters for structured recording, skill evolution foundation for self-improving skills.\n- **Orchestration overhaul** — Harness audit scoring made deterministic, orchestration status and launcher compatibility hardened, observer loop prevention with 5-layer guard.\n- **Observer reliability** — Memory explosion fix with throttling and tail sampling, sandbox access fix, lazy-start logic, and re-entrancy guard.\n- **12 language ecosystems** — New rules for Java, PHP, Perl, Kotlin/Android/KMP, C++, and Rust join existing TypeScript, Python, Go, and common rules.\n- **Community contributions** — Korean and Chinese translations, security hook, biome hook optimization, video processing skills, operational skills, PowerShell installer, Antigravity IDE support.\n- **CI hardening** — 19 test failure fixes, catalog count enforcement, install manifest validation, and full test suite green.\n\n### v1.8.0 — Harness Performance System (Mar 2026)\n\n- **Harness-first release** — ECC is now explicitly framed as an agent harness performance system, not just a config pack.\n- **Hook reliability overhaul** — SessionStart root fallback, Stop-phase session summaries, and script-based hooks replacing fragile inline one-liners.\n- **Hook runtime controls** — &#96;ECC_HOOK_PROFILE=minimal|standard|strict&#96; and &#96;ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS=...&#96; for runtime gating without editing hook files.\n- **New harness commands** — &#96;/harness-audit&#96;, &#96;/loop-start&#96;, &#96;/loop-status&#96;, &#96;/quality-gate&#96;, &#96;/model-route&#96;.\n- **NanoClaw v2** — model routing, skill hot-load, session branch/search/export/compact/metrics.\n- **Cross-harness parity** — behavior tightened across Claude Code, Cursor, OpenCode, and Codex app/CLI.\n- **997 internal tests passing** — full suite green after hook/runtime refactor and compatibility updates.\n\n### v1.7.0 — Cross-Platform Expansion \u0026 Presentation Builder (Feb 2026)\n\n- **Codex app + CLI support** — Direct &#96;AGENTS.md&#96;-based Codex support, installer targeting, and Codex docs\n- **&#96;frontend-slides&#96; skill** — Zero-dependency HTML presentation builder with PPTX conversion guidance and strict viewport-fit rules\n- **5 new generic business/content skills** — &#96;article-writing&#96;, &#96;content-engine&#96;, &#96;market-research&#96;, &#96;investor-materials&#96;, &#96;investor-outreach&#96;\n- **Broader tool coverage** — Cursor, Codex, and OpenCode support tightened so the same repo ships cleanly across all major harnesses\n- **992 internal tests** — Expanded validation and regression coverage across plugin, hooks, skills, and packaging\n\n### v1.6.0 — Codex CLI, AgentShield \u0026 Marketplace (Feb 2026)\n\n- **Codex CLI support** — New &#96;/codex-setup&#96; command generates &#96;codex.md&#96; for OpenAI Codex CLI compatibility\n- **7 new skills** — &#96;search-first&#96;, &#96;swift-actor-persistence&#96;, &#96;swift-protocol-di-testing&#96;, &#96;regex-vs-llm-structured-text&#96;, &#96;content-hash-cache-pattern&#96;, &#96;cost-aware-llm-pipeline&#96;, &#96;skill-stocktake&#96;\n- **AgentShield integration** — &#96;/security-scan&#96; skill runs AgentShield directly from Claude Code; 1282 tests, 102 rules\n- **GitHub Marketplace** — ECC Tools GitHub App live at [github.com/marketplace/ecc-tools](https://github.com/marketplace/ecc-tools) with free/pro/enterprise tiers\n- **30+ community PRs merged** — Contributions from 30 contributors across 6 languages\n- **978 internal tests** — Expanded validation suite across agents, skills, commands, hooks, and rules\n\n### v1.4.1 — Bug Fix (Feb 2026)\n\n- **Fixed instinct import content loss** — &#96;parse_instinct_file()&#96; was silently dropping all content after frontmatter (Action, Evidence, Examples sections) during &#96;/instinct-import&#96;. ([#148](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/issues/148), [#161](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/pull/161))\n\n### v1.4.0 — Multi-Language Rules, Installation Wizard \u0026 PM2 (Feb 2026)\n\n- **Interactive installation wizard** — New &#96;configure-ecc&#96; skill provides guided setup with merge/overwrite detection\n- **PM2 \u0026 multi-agent orchestration** — 6 new commands (&#96;/pm2&#96;, &#96;/multi-plan&#96;, &#96;/multi-execute&#96;, &#96;/multi-backend&#96;, &#96;/multi-frontend&#96;, &#96;/multi-workflow&#96;) for managing complex multi-service workflows\n- **Multi-language rules architecture** — Rules restructured from flat files into &#96;common/&#96; + &#96;typescript/&#96; + &#96;python/&#96; + &#96;golang/&#96; directories. Install only the languages you need\n- **Chinese (zh-CN) translations** — Complete translation of all agents, commands, skills, and rules (80+ files)\n- **GitHub Sponsors support** — Sponsor the project via GitHub Sponsors\n- **Enhanced CONTRIBUTING.md** — Detailed PR templates for each contribution type\n\n### v1.3.0 — OpenCode Plugin Support (Feb 2026)\n\n- **Full OpenCode integration** — 12 agents, 24 commands, 16 skills with hook support via OpenCode's plugin system (20+ event types)\n- **3 native custom tools** — run-tests, check-coverage, security-audit\n- **LLM documentation** — &#96;llms.txt&#96; for comprehensive OpenCode docs\n\n### v1.2.0 — Unified Commands \u0026 Skills (Feb 2026)\n\n- **Python/Django support** — Django patterns, security, TDD, and verification skills\n- **Java Spring Boot skills** — Patterns, security, TDD, and verification for Spring Boot\n- **Session management** — &#96;/sessions&#96; command for session history\n- **Continuous learning v2** — Instinct-based learning with confidence scoring, import/export, evolution\n\nSee the full changelog in [Releases](https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code/releases).\n\n---\n\n## 🚀 Quick Start\n\nGet up and running in under 2 minutes:\n\n### Step 1: Install the Plugin\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Add marketplace\n/plugin marketplace add affaan-m/everything-claude-code\n\n# Install plugin\n/plugin install everything-claude-code@everything-claude-code\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 2: Install Rules (Required)\n\n\u003e ⚠️ **Important:** Claude Code plugins cannot distribute &#96;rules&#96; automatically. Install them manually:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Clone the repo first\ngit clone https://github.com/affaan-m/everything-claude-code.git\ncd everything-claude-code\n\n# Install dependencies (pick your package manager)\nnpm install        # or: pnpm install | yarn install | bun install\n\n# macOS/Linux\n./install.sh typescript    # or python or golang or swift or php\n# ./install.sh typescript python golang swift php\n# ./install.sh --target cursor typescript\n# ./install.sh --target antigravity typescript\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;powershell\n# Windows PowerShell\n.\\install.ps1 typescript   # or python or golang or swift or php\n# .\\install.ps1 typescript python golang swift php\n# .\\install.ps1 --target cursor typescript\n# .\\install.ps1 --target antigravity typescript\n\n# npm-installed compatibility entrypoint also works cross-platform\nnpx ecc-install typescript\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nFor manual install instructions see the README in the &#96;rules/&#96; folder.\n\n### Step 3: Start Using\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Try a command (plugin install uses namespaced form)\n/everything-claude-code:plan \"Add user authentication\"\n\n# Manual install (Option 2) uses the shorter form:\n# /plan \"Add user authentication\"\n\n# Check available commands\n/plugin list everything-claude-code@everything-claude-code\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n✨ **That's it!** You now have access to 28 agents, 119 skills, and 60 commands.\n\n---\n\n## 🌐 Cross-Platform Support\n\nThis plugin now fully supports **Windows, macOS, and Linux**, alongside tight integration across major IDEs (Cursor, OpenCode, Antigravity) and CLI harnesses. All hooks and scripts have been rewritten in Node.js for maximum compatibility.\n\n### Package Manager Detection\n\nThe plugin automatically detects your preferred package manager (npm, pnpm, yarn, or bun) with the following priority:\n\n1. **Environment variable**: &#96;CLAUDE_PACKAGE_MANAGER&#96;\n2. **Project config**: &#96;.claude/package-manager.json&#96;\n3. **package.json**: &#96;packageManager&#96; field\n4. **Lock file**: Detection from package-lock.json, yarn.lock, pnpm-lock.yaml, or bun.lockb\n5. **Global config**: &#96;~/.claude/package-manager.json&#96;\n6. **Fallback**: First available package manager\n\nTo set your preferred package manager:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Via environment variable\nexport CLAUDE_PACKAGE_MANAGER=pnpm\n\n# Via global config\nnode scripts/setup-package-manager.js --global pnpm\n\n# Via project config\nnode scripts/setup-package-manager.js --project bun\n\n# Detect current setting\nnode scripts/setup-package-manager.js --detect\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOr use the &#96;/setup-pm&#96; command in Claude Code.\n\n### Hook Runtime Controls\n\nUse runtime flags to tune strictness or disable specific hooks temporarily:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# Hook strictness profile (default: standard)\nexport ECC_HOOK_PROFILE=standard\n\n# Comma-separated hook IDs to disable\nexport ECC_DISABLED_HOOKS=\"pre:bash:tmux-reminder,post:edit:typecheck\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n---\n\n## 📦 What's Inside\n\nThis repo is a **Claude Code plugin** - install it directly or copy components manually.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\neverything-claude-code/\n|-- .claude-plugin/   # Plugin and marketplace manifests\n|   |-- plugin.json         # Plugin metadata and component paths\n|   |-- marketplace.json    # Marketplace catalog for /plugin marketplace add\n|\n|-- agents/           #
